stocks.note: Filed Chapter 11 Nov 29 2011; stock fell to ~20 cents and traded as AAMRQ. Unusually, common holders recovered value via the Dec 2013 merger with US Airways into American Airlines Group (AAL), receiving AAL shares on emergence.
Pharmaceutical distributor; renamed Cencora and changed NYSE ticker from ABC to COR effective Aug 30 2023. Same company continues.
Bond insurer crushed by the financial crisis; from ~$90 in 2006-07 to pennies. Filed Chapter 11 on Nov 8 2010; old common (ABKFQ) cancelled. Reorganized entity later traded as AMBC with new shares.
Heart-pump (Impella) maker acquired by Johnson & Johnson for $380.00/share cash plus a non-tradeable CVR of up to $35/share; tender closed Dec 21 2022, deal completed Dec 22 2022.

BDC acquired by Ares Capital (ARCC), completed Jan 3 2017. Holders received ~$18.06 total per share: 0.483 ARCC shares plus roughly $9 in cash (ARCC cash, AMC Mortgage Mgmt sale proceeds, and Ares support payment).
BPO/IT services firm acquired by Xerox; each Class A share converted to 4.935 XRX shares plus $18.60 cash (~$63.11/share at announcement). Merger completed Feb 5 2010.

stocks.company_name: Alliance Data Systems Corporation (later Bread Financial Holdings)
stocks.note: Loyalty/private-label credit firm; renamed Bread Financial Holdings and changed NYSE ticker from ADS to BFH effective Apr 4 2022 (after the LoyaltyOne/Air Miles spinoff). Same company continues.
Canadian aluminum major acquired by Rio Tinto for US$101.00/share cash (~$38B) after topping an Alcoa hostile bid; tender offer closed Nov 8 2007 with ~98% tendered.
Acquired by AstraZeneca for $60 cash plus 2.1243 AstraZeneca ADSs per share (~$175 total at announcement); deal closed July 2021.
Acquired by MACOM for $3.25 cash plus 0.1089 MACOM shares (~$8.36) per share, Jan 2017; early-2001 anchor is split-adjusted for the Dec-2007 1-for-4 reverse split.
Acquired by CommScope for $15.00/share (final mix $13.50 cash + $1.50 in CommScope stock), closed late December 2007.
Acquired by Synopsys for $197 cash plus 0.345 Synopsys shares per share (~$35B deal); closed July 17, 2025.
Acquired by Schneider Electric for $31.00/share cash (~$6.1B); announced Oct 2006, closed Feb 14, 2007.

stocks.company_name: Apollo Education Group, Inc. (Apollo Group / University of Phoenix)
stocks.note: Taken private by Apollo Global Management / Vistria / Najafi consortium for $9.50/share cash; closed Feb 1, 2017.
Acquired by Air Liquide for $143.00/share cash (~$13.4B); announced Nov 2015, closed May 23, 2016.
Multifamily REIT taken private by Tishman Speyer / Lehman Brothers partnership for $60.75/share cash (~$22B); closed Oct 5, 2007.
Specialty metals maker; traded NYSE as ATI/AT, rebranded to ATI Inc. (same ticker ATI) in 2021; still active. Highly cyclical, peaked near $115 in 2007.

stocks.company_name: Adtalem Global Education Inc. (formerly DeVry Education Group)
stocks.note: DeVry Education (ticker DV) renamed Adtalem Global Education, ticker ATGE, on 2017-05-24; still actively trades on NYSE, near record highs in 2025-26.
Avon acquired by Brazil's Natura &Co in all-share deal closed 2020-01-03; 0.300 Natura &Co ADS (NTCO) per Avon share.
Allied Waste merged into Republic Services (RSG) closed Dec 2008; 0.45 Republic share per Allied share; Allied holders ended with ~51.7% of combined company.
Spun from AT&T in 2001; acquired by Cingular Wireless (SBC/BellSouth JV) for $15.00 cash per share, closed Oct 2004.
Utility acquired by FirstEnergy (FE) in all-stock deal closed 2011-02-25; 0.667 FirstEnergy share per Allegheny share. Near-bankruptcy energy-trading collapse in 2002 caused deep dip.
Medical device maker acquired by Becton Dickinson (BDX) for ~$317/share, closed 2017-12-29: $222.93 cash plus 0.5077 BDX share per Bard share.
Black & Decker merged with The Stanley Works (SWK) to form Stanley Black & Decker, closed 2010-03-12; 1.275 Stanley shares per B&D share.
Legacy Biogen merged with IDEC Pharmaceuticals (Nov 2003); 1.15 IDEC shares per Biogen share; combined firm became Biogen Idec, IDPH ticker changed to BIIB.

Filed Chapter 11 Sept 9 2024, delisted from NYSE (moved to OTC as BIGGQ); going-concern assets sold to Variety Wholesalers via Gordon Brothers; common shareholders wiped out, later converted to Chapter 7.
Acquired by Baker Hughes (closed Apr 28 2010); each BJS share received $2.69 cash plus 0.40035 Baker Hughes shares (mixed cash/stock deal).
Same company continues; ticker changed from BLL to BALL on NYSE effective May 10 2022 (50th anniversary as public company). Note: 2-for-1 splits in 2016/2017 affect split-adjusted levels.
Acquired by AT&T (closed Dec 29 2006); each BellSouth share converted into 1.325 AT&T shares (~$47 value at close).
Taken private at $46.00/share cash by a private-equity consortium (Blackstone, Goldman Sachs Capital, KKR, TPG); LBO completed Sept 25 2007.
Acquired by Berkshire Hathaway for $100/share (cash-or-stock election; valued at $100) for the 77.4% not already owned; deal completed Feb 12 2010.

Acquired by Avago Technologies for ~$54.50/share (cash-and-stock; merged entity renamed Broadcom Limited, ticker AVGO); closed Feb 1 2016. Elections were $54.50 cash or 0.4378 AVGO shares per share; default mix shown.
Generic drugmaker acquired by Teva for $39.90 cash plus 0.6272 Teva ADR per share (~$66.50 implied), closed Dec 23, 2008.
Investment bank collapsed in March 2008; acquired by JPMorgan at revised ~$10/share (0.21753 JPM shares per BSC share), closed May 2008.
Largest US coal producer; filed Chapter 11 April 2016, old BTUUQ shares cancelled for no value April 3, 2017 (prices split-adjusted for Sept 2015 1-for-15 reverse split).
Biopharma spun off from Baxter July 2015; acquired by Shire for $18.00 cash plus 0.1482 Shire ADS per share (~$45.57 implied), closed June 3, 2016.
Retail bank acquired by TD Bank Financial Group for $10.50 cash plus 0.4142 TD shares per share (~$42 implied), closed March 31, 2008.
Media company; merged with Viacom Dec 4, 2019, with CBS as surviving entity renamed ViacomCBS (CBS shares became VIAC 1:1; Viacom shares converted at 0.59625).
Alabama-based bank acquired by BBVA in 2007; holders elected $71.82 cash or 2.8 BBVA ADS per share (subject to proration), closed Sept 2007.
Consumer-electronics retailer; filed Chapter 11 Nov 10, 2008, announced liquidation Jan 16, 2009, common shares rendered worthless.
Ceridian rebranded to Dayforce; ticker changed from CDAY to DAY on NYSE/TSX effective Feb 1, 2024; same company continues, no shareholder action.
Acquired by Bristol-Myers Squibb Nov 20, 2019: each CELG share got 1.0 BMY share + $50.00 cash + one tradeable CVR (potential $9 on milestones). Prices split-adjusted.
Acquired by Teva Pharmaceutical for $81.50/share cash (~$6.8B); deal announced May 2, 2011, completed Oct 14, 2011, delisted from NASDAQ.
Spun off from Cardinal Health Sept 2009. Acquired by Becton Dickinson Mar 17, 2015: each CFN share got $49.00 cash + 0.0777 BDX share (~$58 total).

CITGO (formerly Cities Service) was a wholly/majority owned subsidiary of Venezuela's PDVSA with minimal public float; security withdrawn from NYSE listing per SEC delisting order around Feb 2006. Prices are rough estimates of thin residual minority shares; not a freely-traded large cap by this period.
All-stock acquisition by Fifth Third Bancorp (announced Oct 6, 2025, closed ~Feb 1, 2026): each CMA share got 1.8663 FITB shares (~$82.88 implied at announcement). Comerica delisted.

Conoco Inc. (Class B shares, largely held by former parent DuPont) merged with Phillips Petroleum to form ConocoPhillips Aug 30, 2002; each Conoco share received 0.4677 ConocoPhillips (COP) shares. Began trading as COP Sept 3, 2002.

Cabot Oil & Gas was the surviving legal entity in the merger-of-equals with Cimarex Energy; renamed Coterra Energy, ticker changed from COG to CTRA effective Oct 4, 2021. Existing COG shares continued 1:1 as CTRA.
Spun off from Tyco in 2007; acquired by Medtronic Jan 2015 for $35.19 cash plus 0.956 Medtronic plc shares per Covidien share (~$93+ value).
Spun off from NiSource July 2015; acquired by TransCanada (TC Energy) July 2016 for $25.50 per share in cash.
Calpine filed Chapter 11 Dec 2005; old common (pink sheets CPNLQ) cancelled on emergence Jan 2008. Reorganized company relisted on NYSE as CPN.
Acquired by Goodyear June 2021 for $41.75 cash plus 0.907 Goodyear shares per Cooper share (~$54-58 implied value).
IPO July 2014; ran up to ~$140 in 2021 on COVID/CDMO demand, then fell. Acquired by Novo Holdings Dec 2024 for $63.50 per share cash.
Homebuilder; merged into Pulte Homes Aug 2009 at 0.975 Pulte shares per Centex share. Combined company became PulteGroup (PHM).
Dolan family cable operator; acquired by Altice June 2016 for $34.90 per share cash. Became part of Altice USA.
Permian shale producer; peaked ~$150 in 2018, hit ~$40 in 2020 oil crash. Acquired by ConocoPhillips Jan 2021 in all-stock deal at 1.46 COP shares per Concho share.

stocks.company_name: Delta Air Lines, Inc. (pre-2007 bankruptcy common stock)
stocks.note: Old Delta common (DALRQ) was canceled when Delta exited Chapter 11 in spring 2007; new DAL stock issued. Pre-bankruptcy shares wiped out.
Consumer-products maker (Dial soap, Purex) acquired by Germany's Henkel for $28.75/share cash, closed March 29, 2004.
Auto-parts maker filed Chapter 11 March 3, 2006; old common (DCNAQ) canceled when Dana emerged Jan/Feb 2008 as new Dana Holding (DAN). Pre-bankruptcy equity wiped out.
Largest US milk processor filed Chapter 11 Nov 12, 2019; assets sold (mostly to Dairy Farmers of America). Common stock canceled, shareholders wiped out.
Credit-card issuer/network; IPO June 2007. Acquired by Capital One in all-stock deal closing May 18, 2025; 1.0192 COF shares per DFS share.

stocks.company_name: Discovery Communications, Inc. / Discovery, Inc. (Class A)
stocks.note: Discovery merged with AT&T's WarnerMedia, became Warner Bros. Discovery on Apr 8, 2022; each DISCA share converted 1:1 to WBD common stock.

stocks.company_name: Discovery Communications, Inc. / Discovery, Inc. (Class C)
stocks.note: Non-voting Class C of Discovery; converted 1:1 into Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) common at the Apr 8, 2022 merger close. Traded slightly below DISCA.
Publisher of The Wall Street Journal; acquired by Rupert Murdoch's News Corp for $60.00/share cash, closed Dec 13, 2007. Stock jumped from ~$36 to ~$56 on the May 1, 2007 bid.

Oil & gas E&P; filed Chapter 11 on 2020-07-30 amid the oil crash, existing common stock cancelled and NYSE suspended trading (moved to OTC as DNRCQ); company reorganized as Denbury Inc (DEN), later bought by ExxonMobil 2023, but the DNR common was wiped out.

Auto-parts maker spun off from GM in 1999; filed Chapter 11 on 2005-10-08, closed at $0.33 on 2005-10-10 before NYSE suspended trading; traded on OTC as DPHIQ and old common was ultimately wiped out in the 2009 reorganization.

Industrial/logistics REIT acquired by Prologis in an all-stock deal that closed 2022-10-03; Duke shareholders received 0.475 Prologis (PLD) shares per DRE share, valued near $62/share at announcement.

Holding company from the 2017 Dow-DuPont merger; spun off Dow Inc (DOW) in April 2019 and Corteva (CTVA) on 2019-06-01, then was renamed DuPont de Nemours and the ticker continued as DD (post-spin/reverse-split price levels differ).
IT services giant founded by Ross Perot; acquired by Hewlett-Packard for $25.00/share cash (about $13.9B), deal announced May 2008 and closed 2008-08-26; became HP Enterprise Services.

Iconic photography/imaging company (ticker EK); filed Chapter 11 on 2012-01-19 after shares fell below $1, delisted from NYSE and old common (OTC as EKDKQ) rendered worthless; reissued as KODK upon 2013 emergence.

Specialty/generic pharma (formerly Endo Health, Endo Pharmaceuticals); filed Chapter 11 on 2022-08-16 under opioid-litigation pressure, shares fell to cents and were delisted, equity holders received little or nothing.
Largest US office REIT, run by Sam Zell; taken private by Blackstone for $55.50/share cash (~$39B) after a bidding war with Vornado, deal closed 2007-02-07; one of the largest LBOs ever at the time.
Ensco plc combined with Rowan (Apr 11, 2019), becoming Ensco Rowan plc and later renamed Valaris plc (VAL); a 1-for-4 reverse split occurred at close. Ensco shares continued as the surviving entity.

Acquired by Morgan Stanley in an all-stock deal completed Oct 2, 2020; E*TRADE holders received 1.0432 MS shares per ETFC share (~$58.74 announce-date value). Note 2007-09 financial-crisis collapse and a 2010 1-for-10 reverse split (figures split-adjusted).
Acquired by Bank of America, effective Apr 1, 2004; FleetBoston holders received 0.5553 BAC shares per FBF share (~$45/share, ~$47B deal).
Spun off MasterBrand cabinets and renamed Fortune Brands Innovations (ticker changed FBHS -> FBIN) effective Dec 15, 2022; same company continues. Spun off from Fortune Brands in Oct 2011.
Re-IPO'd Oct 2015 (was KKR-owned 2007-2015); acquired by Fiserv in an all-stock deal completed July 29, 2019 at 0.303 FISV per FDC share. Final close $31.69 on 2019-07-26.
Acquired by Dollar Tree, closed July 6, 2015; cash-and-stock: $59.60 cash plus 0.2475 DLTR shares per FDO share (~$74.50-79 total value).

Fiserv (long traded NASDAQ:FISV) moved to NYSE and changed ticker to FI in June 2023; same company. Reverted to NASDAQ:FISV in Nov 2025. Stock collapsed in 2025-2026 (52-wk range ~$47-176; ~$49 in June 2026). Prices split-adjusted (multiple 2:1 splits).
Federated Investors renamed Federated Hermes; ticker changed FII -> FHI, began trading as FHI on Feb 3, 2020. Same company continues.
Acquired by Teledyne Technologies for $28.00 cash plus 0.0718 Teledyne shares per FLIR share (~$57/sh), closed May 14, 2021.
Rebranded to Corpay; ticker changed from FLT to CPAY on the NYSE effective March 25, 2024; same company continues trading.
Acquired by Actavis plc for $26.04 cash plus 0.3306 Actavis shares (~$89/sh) per Forest share, closed July 1, 2014.
Acquired by NXP Semiconductors for $6.25 cash plus 0.3521 NXP shares per Freescale share (~$42/sh), closed Dec 7, 2015. (IPO'd 2011.)

Filed Chapter 11 on Apr 14, 2020; plan confirmed Aug 21, 2020 gave equity to bondholders, old common cancelled with no recovery. Prices are split-adjusted for the 1-for-15 reverse split (mid-2017) so pre-2017 anchors reflect post-split equivalents.

stocks.company_name: The Great Atlantic & Pacific Tea Company, Inc. (A&P)
stocks.note: Great Atlantic & Pacific Tea Co (A&P) filed Chapter 11 on Dec 12, 2010; stock dropped under $1 and was delisted (became GAPTQ); common wiped out, emerged private in 2012, liquidated in 2015.

General Growth Properties acquired by Brookfield Property Partners Aug 2018; shareholders elected $23.50 cash or 0.9656 BPY units per share (subject to proration). Note: 2009 Ch.11 reorg returned value via new GGP shares plus Howard Hughes Corp spinoff; anchors reflect the post-reorg continuing entity.

stocks.company_name: Keurig Green Mountain, Inc. (formerly Green Mountain Coffee Roasters)
stocks.note: Keurig Green Mountain (formerly Green Mountain Coffee Roasters) taken private by JAB Holding-led group for $92.00 cash per share (~$13.9B), closed Mar 3, 2016.
PC maker acquired by Acer via $1.90/share cash tender offer; NYSE trading suspended Oct 16, 2007.
Innerwear/activewear maker (Sara Lee spinoff 2006); acquired by Gildan Activewear for $0.80 cash + 0.102 GIL shares per share; delisted Dec 1, 2025. (2:1 split adjusted ~2013 reflected in anchors.)
NJ thrift acquired by M&T Bank; mixed consideration averaging ~60% cash ($10.06/sh) and 40% stock (0.08403 MTB/sh); effective Nov 1, 2015.
Nursing/skilled-care operator taken private by The Carlyle Group for $67.00/share cash (~$6.3B); closed Dec 21, 2007.

Integrated oil & gas (formerly Amerada Hess); acquired by Chevron in all-stock deal, 1.0250 CVX per HES share; closed July 18, 2025. (2006 anchor reflects pre-split; HES did a 2:1 split? No split—2005/06 spike from secondary; magnitudes approximate.)

Refiner (Holly Corp merged with Frontier Oil 2011); reorganized into HF Sinclair after Sinclair acquisition, ticker changed HFC->DINO Mar 15, 2022. Early-2000s anchors are split-adjusted (multiple splits).
Consumer finance giant; shares hit by 2002 predatory-lending settlement; acquired by HSBC for 0.535 HSBC ADS (or 2.675 ordinary shares) per share; closed Mar 28, 2003.
Rural/community hospital operator; acquired by Community Health Systems for $10.50 cash + 0.06942 CYH shares + a CVR (up to $1.00); closed Jan 27, 2014.
Acquired by Berkshire Hathaway and 3G Capital for $72.50/share cash; deal closed June 2013.
Taken private by Blackstone and TPG for $76/share cash plus up to $3/share CVR; deal closed April 2026.
Merged with L3 Technologies; Harris renamed L3Harris Technologies and ticker changed from HRS to LHX July 2019 (Harris holders kept shares 1:1).
Acquired by Pfizer for $90/share cash; deal closed September 2015. (Spun off from Abbott in 2004.)
Acquired by GTECH S.p.A.; holders got $14.34 cash + 0.1819 shares of new UK holding co, which then trades under the IGT ticker. Deal closed April 2015.
Acquired by Amgen; each Immunex share converted to 0.44 Amgen shares plus $4.50 cash. Deal closed July 2002.
Canadian nickel miner acquired by CVRD (Vale) for C$86/share cash (~US$77) in late 2006; common subsequently delisted. (INCLF was the US/foreign-shares quote.)

stocks.company_name: The Interpublic Group of Companies, Inc.
stocks.note: Acquired by Omnicom Group in an all-stock deal; each IPG share converted to 0.344 Omnicom (OMC) shares. Deal closed late November 2025; IPG delisted from NYSE.
Filed Chapter 11 on May 15, 2020; NYSE delisted, shares moved to OTC as JCPNQ and were ultimately canceled, leaving common shareholders with nothing.
Changed NYSE ticker from JEC to J on Dec 10, 2019 as part of rebrand; same company later reorganized as Jacobs Solutions Inc. (2022) keeping ticker J, still trades today.
Acquired by Manulife Financial; each John Hancock share converted to 1.1853 Manulife (MFC) shares, deal closed and stock stopped trading Apr 28, 2004.
Acquired by Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) for $40.00/share all cash, ~$14B; deal closed July 2, 2025 after DOJ antitrust settlement.
Merger of equals with Henderson Group to form Janus Henderson Group (JHG); each Janus share converted to 0.4719 JHG shares (post 1-for-10 consolidation), closed late May 2017.

stocks.company_name: The Jones Group Inc. (formerly Jones Apparel Group)
stocks.note: Acquired by Sycamore Partners for $15.00/share cash (~$2.2B incl. debt); deal closed April 8, 2014.

stocks.company_name: Joy Global Inc. (formerly Joy Mining / Harnischfeger)
stocks.note: Acquired by Komatsu for $28.30/share cash (~$3.7B incl. debt); deal closed April 5, 2017, became Komatsu Mining Corp.
Merged into Lincoln National (LNC) in April 2006; holders elected either $55.96 cash or 1.0906 LNC shares per share, subject to a $1.8B aggregate cash cap (mostly stock).

stocks.company_name: Kate Spade & Company (formerly Liz Claiborne / Fifth & Pacific)
stocks.note: Kate Spade & Co (renamed from Liz Claiborne/Fifth & Pacific; ticker KATE from 2014) acquired by Coach Inc for $18.50/share cash, closed July 2017.
Michael Kors Holdings renamed Capri Holdings on Dec 31 2018 after buying Versace; ticker changed KORS to CPRI on Jan 2 2019, same company continues.
MBNA (credit card issuer) acquired by Bank of America Jan 1 2006; each share got 0.5009 BAC shares plus $4.125 cash (~$27.50 total).
Kraft Foods Group (spun from Mondelez Oct 2012) merged with H.J. Heinz to form Kraft Heinz July 2015; 1 KHC share per KRFT share plus $16.50 special dividend.
KeySpan (NY/New England gas & electric utility) acquired by UK's National Grid for $42.00/share cash; announced Feb 2006, closed Aug 24 2007.
Kansas City Southern railroad acquired by Canadian Pacific (CP, now CPKC) Dec 14 2021; each KSU share got $90 cash plus 2.884 CP shares (~$300 value).
Lehman Brothers, 4th-largest US investment bank, filed largest-ever US bankruptcy Sept 15 2008; common stock wiped out (ticker LEH became LEHMQ). 2001-2006 prices split-adjusted for 2:1 split.
Linear Technology (analog semiconductor maker) acquired by Analog Devices March 2017; each LLTC share got $46.00 cash plus 0.2321 ADI shares (~$60 total).
Asset manager acquired by Franklin Resources (BEN) for $50.00/share all-cash; deal announced Feb 2020, closed Jul 31, 2020.

Cigarette maker (spun off from Loews June 2008) acquired by Reynolds American; holders got $50.50 cash + 0.2909 RAI share (~$68.88 total). Prices split-adjusted for Jan 2013 3-for-1 split; closed Jun 12, 2015.
Semiconductor/storage chip maker (renamed from LSI Logic after Apr 2007 Agere merger) acquired by Avago Technologies (AVGO) for $11.15/share all-cash; closed May 6, 2014.

Fiber/telecom carrier acquired by CenturyLink (CTL, now Lumen); holders got $26.50 cash + 1.4286 CTL shares per share. Pre-Oct-2011 anchors split-adjusted (x15) for the 1-for-15 reverse split for internal consistency; closed Nov 1, 2017.
Printer maker (spun off from IBM) taken private by a consortium led by Apex Technology and PAG Asia Capital for $40.50/share all-cash; closed Nov 29, 2016.

Media/magazine company. On Dec 1, 2021 the Local Media (TV) business was sold to Gray Television (GTN) for $16.99/share cash; immediately prior, the magazine/digital business was spun off and acquired by IAC's Dotdash (~$42.18/share value), so total realized value per share ~$59.17. Modeled as cash buyout for the Gray cash leg; see note for spinoff value.

Energy/engineering (offshore oil & gas EPC). Filed prepackaged Chapter 11 Jan 21, 2020; existing common cancelled (shareholders got nothing); delisted from NYSE Feb 2020. Last trade ~$0.60. Pre-2018 anchors are approximate and split-adjusted-equivalent (3-for-2 2006, 2-for-1 2007 splits, then 1-for-3 reverse split May 2018); magnitudes approximate, hence low confidence.

Paper/forest-products company merged with Westvaco to form MeadWestvaco (MWV) on Jan 29, 2002; Mead holders received 1.0 MWV share per Mead share plus a $1.20/share cash payment. Stock traded only briefly in the 2001 window before the merger.
Merged with Bank of New York to form Bank of New York Mellon (BK); each Mellon share became 1 share of the new company.
Acquired by Hewlett-Packard via cash tender offer at $52.00 per share; deal closed November 2006.
Spun off from Bristol-Myers Squibb in 2009 (IPO ~$24); acquired by Reckitt Benckiser for $90.00 cash per share, closed mid-2017.
Marsh & McLennan (insurance brokerage / professional services) remains an active S&P 500 constituent trading under MMC; temporarily unfetchable.

stocks.company_name: Motors Liquidation Company (formerly General Motors Corporation)
stocks.note: Old General Motors filed Chapter 11 on 2009-06-01; common renamed Motors Liquidation (MTLQQ), shares cancelled worthless in 2011. New GM is a separate company.
Merged with Rock-Tenn to form WestRock (WRK); each MWV share converted to 0.78 WestRock shares, closed July 2015.
Online job-search company (Monster.com); acquired by Randstad via cash tender offer at $3.40 per share, completed October 2016.
Analog semiconductor maker; acquired by Analog Devices (ADI) in all-stock deal, 0.63 ADI shares per MXIM share, closed August 26, 2021.
Appliance maker acquired by Whirlpool for $21/share (cash-and-stock, mostly cash) completed March 31, 2006.

stocks.company_name: Mylan N.V. (formerly Mylan Inc. / Mylan Laboratories)
stocks.note: Generic-drug maker; combined with Pfizer's Upjohn via Reverse Morris Trust to form Viatris (VTRS) on Nov 16, 2020; former Mylan holders' shares became Viatris shares 1:1.
Truck and engine maker acquired by TRATON SE (Volkswagen Group) for $44.50/share cash; deal completed July 1, 2021.
Oil & gas E&P acquired by Chevron in all-stock deal; each NBL share converted to 0.1191 CVX shares; closed Oct 5, 2020.
Split into two companies Oct 16, 2023: NCR Corp renamed NCR Voyix (VYX) and spun off NCR Atleos (NATL, 1 share per 2 NCR shares). Pre-split NCR continues as VYX.
New York bank acquired by Capital One (cash-or-stock election: $11.25 cash plus 0.2216 COF per share, ~$31.18/sh at signing); closed Dec 1, 2006.

Symantec sold enterprise unit to Broadcom in 2019 and renamed NortonLifeLock (NLOK); after acquiring Avast, rebranded as Gen Digital (GEN) effective Nov 8, 2022. Pre-2019 anchors reflect Symantec under same ticker SYMC.
Networking/software firm acquired by Attachmate for $6.10/share cash (~$2.2B); concurrent patent sale to CPTN (Microsoft-led); closed April 27, 2011. Acquirer was private.
Telecom-equipment maker filed Chapter 11/CCAA Jan 2009; assets sold off, common stock rendered worthless. Prices split-adjusted for the Dec 2006 1-for-10 reverse split.
Merged with Sprint to form Sprint Nextel on Aug 12, 2005; each Nextel share converted to 1.2675 Sprint shares plus ~$0.85 cash.
Operator of NYSE acquired by IntercontinentalExchange (ICE) Nov 13, 2013; holders could elect $33.12 cash, 0.2581 ICE shares, or a mix. NYX began trading 2007 after NYSE-Euronext combination.

stocks.company_name: The ODP Corporation (formerly Office Depot, Inc.)
stocks.note: Office Depot / The ODP Corp still trades on Nasdaq. All pre-July-2020 prices multiplied by 10 for consistency with the 1-for-10 reverse split (Jun 30, 2020); near-bankruptcy lows in 2008-2009 and 2011.
OfficeMax merged with Office Depot Nov 5, 2013; each OMX share converted to 2.69 Office Depot (ODP) shares in an all-stock deal.
Bank One merged into JPMorgan Chase July 1, 2004; each Bank One share converted to 1.32 JPMorgan Chase shares. CEO Jamie Dimon came over in the deal.
Connecticut-based People's United Financial acquired by M&T Bank Apr 1, 2022 in an all-stock deal; each PBCT share converted to 0.118 M&T (MTB) shares. IPO'd in 2007.
Dental/animal-health distributor taken private by Patient Square Capital in 2025 for $31.35/share cash (~$4.1B); shareholders approved Apr 1, 2025, deal closed late April/early May 2025.
Healthpeak (ticker PEAK, formerly HCP) closed merger-of-equals with Physicians Realty Trust and changed ticker to DOC on Mar 4, 2024; 2016 QCP spinoff lowered price level.
Consortium led by BC Partners acquired PetSmart for $83.00/share cash; deal closed Mar 11, 2015.
Peoples Energy merged into WPS Resources (renamed Integrys); each PGL share converted to 0.825 WPS shares, completed Feb 21, 2007.
Pfizer acquired Pharmacia in a tax-free stock swap; each PHA share converted to 1.4 PFE shares, completed Apr 16, 2003.
PerkinElmer rebranded to Revvity, Inc. and ticker changed from PKI to RVTY on May 16, 2023; same continuing company (2002 dip reflects post-bubble decline).
Microsemi acquired PMC-Sierra for $9.22 cash plus 0.0771 MSCC share (~$12.05 total) per PMCS share; closed Jan 15, 2016.
Oracle acquired PeopleSoft for $26.50/share cash after a long hostile tender battle; deal closed early Jan 2005.
ExxonMobil acquired Pioneer Natural Resources in an all-stock deal; each PXD share converted to 2.3234 XOM shares, closed May 3, 2024 (final PXD close ~$269.62).
Acquired by Diamondback Energy in all-stock merger; 0.05 FANG shares per QEP share (~$3.22/sh implied at close), completed Mar 17 2021.
Acquired by Cavium for $11.00 cash plus 0.098 CAVM shares per QLGC share (~$15.50 total); exchange offer completed Aug 2016.
Taken private by founder Dennis Gillings and One Equity Partners (Pharma Services) for $14.50/share cash; completed Sep 25 2003. Later became part of IQVIA.

British American Tobacco bought the ~58% of RAI it did not own for $29.44 cash + 0.5260 BAT ADS per share (~$59.64 total); completed Jul 25 2017. Note: prices reflect post-2010 2-for-1 split adjustments.
Acquired by IBM for $10.50/share cash (~$2.1B), announced Dec 2002, closed Feb 2003; became IBM Rational software brand.
Acquired by Adidas AG for $59.00/share cash (~$3.8B); announced Aug 2005, completed Jan 31 2006. Adidas is foreign-listed, so no US acquirer ticker.
Merged with Ensco to form Ensco Rowan (later Valaris); Rowan holders got 2.750 Ensco shares per RDC share, completed Apr 11 2019. Combined company later did 1-for-4 reverse split.

stocks.company_name: Royal Dutch Shell plc (Class A ADR)
stocks.note: Royal Dutch Shell dropped dual-share structure and 'Royal Dutch' name; RDS.A ADR (multiple A+B lines) consolidated into single Shell plc ADR under ticker SHEL effective Jan 31 2022. Same company continues.

stocks.company_name: Everest Re Group, Ltd. (now Everest Group, Ltd.)
stocks.note: Everest Re Group renamed to Everest Group; NYSE ticker changed RE to EG effective July 10, 2023; same company continues trading.
IBM acquired Red Hat for $190.00/share in cash (~$34B); announced Sept 28, 2018, closed July 9, 2019; stock jumped on announcement.
Dow Chemical acquired Rohm and Haas for $78.00/share in cash (~$19B); announced July 10, 2008, closed April 1, 2009; stock spiked on announcement.

Chatham Asset Management took R.R. Donnelley private for $10.85/share in cash; closed Feb 25, 2022. Note: 2016 spin-offs of LSC Communications and Donnelley Financial create a price discontinuity in as-traded series.
Merger of equals with United Technologies; each RTN share converted to 2.3348 shares of the combined Raytheon Technologies (RTX). Closed April 3, 2020; final price depressed by COVID crash.
Publicis Groupe acquired Sapient for $25.00/share in cash (~$3.7B); announced Nov 3, 2014, completed Feb 6, 2015; stock jumped ~44% on announcement.

Motorola acquired Symbol Technologies for $15.00/share in cash (~$3.9B); announced Sept 19, 2006, closed early January 2007. Stock had been hit by a 2002-2004 accounting fraud scandal. Annual closes approximate.

Oracle acquired Siebel Systems for $10.66/share (~$5.85B); cash with limited stock-election option. Announced Sept 12, 2005, closed Jan 31, 2006. Stock had fallen from a ~$119 peak in late 2000. Annual closes approximate.
Acquired by Clayton, Dubilier & Rice for $42.15/share cash ($10.3B EV), taken private; delisted from NYSE April 9, 2026.
Cable set-top maker acquired by Cisco Systems for $43.00/share cash (~$6.9B); deal closed Feb 24, 2006.
Life-science/chemicals firm acquired by Germany's Merck KGaA for $140.00/share cash (~$17B); closed Nov 18, 2015.
Contract electronics manufacturer acquired by Flextronics ($3.6B); holders elected $3.89 cash or 0.3450 FLEX shares per share; closed Oct 2007.
Cable-TV networks (HGTV, Food Network) acquired by Discovery (~$90/share); holders received $65.82 cash + 1.0584 Discovery Series C shares; closed Mar 6, 2018. Spun off from E.W. Scripps in 2008.

Merger of equals with Pinnacle Financial completed Jan 1, 2026; each SNV share became 0.5237 of new combined company (trades as PNFP). Pre-2014 prices reflect 1-for-7 reverse split (May 2014), so older anchors are scaled x7 for continuity.
Alabama-based bank acquired by Wachovia in all-stock deal (~$14.3B); each SOTR share converted to 0.89 Wachovia shares; closed Nov 1, 2004.
Thrift holding company acquired by Banco Santander during the 2008 crisis (~$3.81/share); each share converted to 0.3206 Santander ADS; closed Jan 30, 2009.
Acquired by Waste Management (WM) for $62.00/share cash, ~$7.2B; deal closed Nov 4, 2024 and SRCL delisted from Nasdaq.
Acquired by Abbott Laboratories (ABT) for ~$85/share: $46.75 cash plus 0.8708 ABT shares per STJ share; closed Jan 4, 2017.
Merged with Chesapeake Energy; each SWN share converted to 0.0867 shares of the combined company, renamed Expand Energy (EXE); closed early Oct 2024.
Acquired by Cerberus-led AB Acquisition (Albertsons) for ~$40/share total: $32.50 cash plus contingent value and Blackhawk stock distribution; closed Jan 30, 2015.
Sold enterprise security business to Broadcom; remaining consumer company renamed NortonLifeLock, ticker changed from SYMC to NLOK on Nov 5, 2019 (later Gen Digital, GEN).
Still trades as TGNA; the $8.6B Standard General buyout was terminated May 22, 2023 after FCC pushback. (Symbol began June 2015 when Gannett split into TEGNA and new Gannett.)
Acquired by LVMH for $131.50/share cash (~$15.8B); deal closed Jan 7, 2021 and TIF delisted from NYSE.
Acquired by International Paper (IP) for $32.00/share cash (~$4.5B incl. debt); closed Feb 13, 2012. Note: spun off Guaranty Financial and Forestar in late 2007.
Torchmark renamed Globe Life Inc.; ticker changed from TMK to GL on Aug 8, 2019, same company continuing.
Taken private by KKR, Bain Capital and Vornado for $26.75/share cash in $6.6B deal; completed July 21, 2005.
Taken private in Sam Zell-led leveraged buyout at $34/share cash ($8.2B); two-step tender, completed Dec 20, 2007 (later bankrupt 2008).
Acquired by Northrop Grumman in all-stock deal; TRW holders received 0.5357 NOC shares per share (~$60 value); closed Dec 2002.
Sabre Holdings taken private by Silver Lake and TPG for $32.75/share cash (~$5B); closed Mar 30, 2007, delisted Apr 2007.
Merged with Global Payments (merger of equals); TSYS holders received 0.8101 GPN shares per share; closed Sept 18, 2019.
Filed Chapter 11 on Sept 17, 2024; assets sold, common equity wiped out; delisted from NYSE, last traded as penny stock late 2024.

Acquired by Charter Communications May 18, 2016; default election ~$100 cash plus ~0.54 CHTR shares per TWC share (alt: $115 cash + 0.4562 CHTR). Note: prices reflect March 2009 1-for-3 reverse split; early-2009 ~$20 low shown pre-recovery.
Texas electric utility taken private in record $45B LBO by KKR/TPG at $69.25/share cash; became Energy Future Holdings.
Traded as 'U' on NYSE; filed Chapter 11 Aug 2002, shares moved to OTC as UAWGQ, old common cancelled when carrier emerged from bankruptcy March 2003.
Dutch arm of Unilever; under 2020 unification each Unilever NV NY registry share converted 1:1 into Unilever PLC ADS (UL); UN delisted from NYSE Dec 2020.

UTC merged with Raytheon (merger of equals) and was renamed Raytheon Technologies (RTX); UTX holders kept shares 1:1 and just before close received 1 Carrier + 0.5 Otis share per UTX share via spinoffs.
Radiation-oncology equipment maker acquired by Siemens Healthineers for $177.50/share cash (~$16.4B); deal closed April 2021.
New Viacom (post-2006 split from old Viacom/CBS); merged back with CBS Dec 2019, each Viacom Class B share converted to 0.59625 ViacomCBS Class B (VIAC) share.
Formed from Dec 2019 CBS-Viacom merger; spiked to ~$100 in Mar 2021 then collapsed in the Archegos unwind; renamed Paramount Global, ticker changed VIAC to PARA Feb 2022.
Ford auto-parts spinoff; filed Chapter 11 May 2009, delisted from NYSE and traded OTC as VSTNQ; old common wiped out on emergence Oct 2010 when reorganized Visteon (VC) issued new shares.
Networking/communications chipmaker acquired by Microsemi for $5.28/share cash tender offer, completed April 2015.
Largest US bank failure; OTS seized WaMu Bank Sep 25, 2008 (sold to JPMorgan), holding company filed Chapter 11 Sep 26; common stock rendered worthless.
Government-sponsored health insurer acquired by Centene for $120 cash plus 3.38 Centene shares per WCG share; deal closed Jan 23, 2020.
Telecom giant collapsed in accounting fraud; filed Chapter 11 Jul 21, 2002, delisted from Nasdaq Jul 30; WCOEQ shares deemed worthless when MCI emerged in 2004.
Organic grocery chain acquired by Amazon for $42.00/share all-cash ($13.7B); announced Jun 16, 2017, closed Aug 28, 2017. (Prices split-adjusted for 2-for-1 splits.)
Oilfield services firm filed prepackaged Chapter 11 Jul 1, 2019; old common (WFT) wiped out, equity went to creditors; reorganized company relisted as WFRD in Dec 2019.

Rural telecom; after adverse court ruling filed Chapter 11 Feb 25, 2019, delisted from Nasdaq, common cancelled in 2020 reorganization. Prices split-adjusted to the 1-for-5 reverse split (May 2018) basis for consistency.

Bakken shale producer filed Chapter 11 Apr 1, 2020 amid oil-price crash; old shareholders got ~1 new share per 75 old (~3% of reorganized equity); old common effectively wiped out. Prices split-adjusted to the 1-for-4 reverse split (late 2017) basis.
WellPoint (formed from Anthem-WellPoint Health Networks 2004 merger) renamed itself Anthem, Inc., ticker WLP changed to ANTM on Dec 3, 2014; later became Elevance Health (ELV) in 2022.

Windstream filed Chapter 11 on Feb 25, 2019 (after losing Aurelius/Uniti covenant ruling); stock crashed to pennies, moved to OTC (WINMQ); existing common equity wiped out on emergence Sep 21, 2020. Prices reflect a 1-for-6 reverse split done in Feb 2018; anchors approximate and roughly split-consistent.
WPX Energy (spun off from Williams, IPO Jan 2012) combined with Devon Energy in all-stock merger of equals; each WPX share converted to 0.5165 Devon (DVN) shares, completed Jan 7, 2021.
WestRock (formed 2015 from MeadWestvaco + RockTenn) combined with Smurfit Kappa to form Smurfit WestRock (SW) on Jul 5, 2024; each WRK share received 1 Smurfit WestRock share plus $5.00 cash.
Wm. Wrigley Jr. Company (chewing gum maker) acquired by privately held Mars, Inc. (with Berkshire Hathaway financing) for $80/share cash; deal announced Apr 28, 2008, closed Oct 6, 2008.
Wyeth (formerly American Home Products) acquired by Pfizer for $33.00 cash plus 0.985 Pfizer (PFE) shares per Wyeth share (~$50.19 total); announced Jan 26, 2009, closed Oct 15, 2009.
U.S. Steel acquired by Japan's Nippon Steel for $55.00/share cash (~$14.1B); deal announced Dec 2023, blocked by Biden, cleared by Trump with a U.S. government golden share, closed Jun 18, 2025.
Cimarex Energy combined with Cabot Oil & Gas in all-stock merger of equals to form Coterra Energy (CTRA); each Cimarex share converted to 4.0146 Cabot/Coterra shares, completed Oct 1, 2021.
All-stock acquisition by ExxonMobil; each XTO share converted to 0.7098 XOM shares; deal announced Dec 14 2009, closed June 25 2010 (~$41B incl. debt).
